It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 burial mounds forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}}== See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum.
