revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 御鷲山古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）/北原古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]薬師寺2142 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御鷲山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]]（下野型古墳） |規模 = 墳丘長83m
高さ5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄鏃・刀子・武具・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=御鷲山古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=24|lat_sec=19.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=52|lon_sec=36.88|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(横塚) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=御鷲山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=下石橋愛宕塚 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=多功大塚山 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=下野薬師寺跡 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''御鷲山古墳'''（おわしやまこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]薬師寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[横塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |'''御鷲山古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、田川西岸の台地上に築造された古墳である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 御鷲山古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。一帯には古墳20基以上から成る北原古墳群が分布し、そのうちで最大規模になる。これまでに墳丘測量調査・石室発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成で、特に1段目は平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（家形埴輪など）・[[須恵器]]（甕）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf しもつけ文化財探訪第13回 御鷲山古墳]}}（下野市）。。埋葬施設は前方部くびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長は12メートルを測り、凝灰岩の巨石の切石積みによって構築された大型石室である。石室内からは鉄鏃・刀子・武具（[[挂甲]]部品）・馬具（辻金具）などが検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。本古墳の南では後世に[[下野薬師寺跡|下野薬師寺]]が営まれており、下野薬師寺の成立を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：第1段（下段）約83メートル、第2段（上段）約63メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約28メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル（第1段上面から） * 前方部 ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル（第1段上面から） == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては前方部くびれ部寄りにおいて[[横穴式石室]]が構築されている。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：約12メートル * 玄室：長さ約2.8メートル、幅約2.0メートル（奥壁）、高さ約2.0メートル 石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]で、巨石の切石積みによって構築される。床面には川原石が敷かれる{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf 御鷲山古墳]}}（下野市）。。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 御鷲山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=御鷲山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|御鷲山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=竹澤謙|year=1989|chapter=御鷲山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|御鷲山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=斎藤恒夫|title=栃木県の前方後円墳ノート2 御鷲山古墳の外形復元|date=2022|journal=栃木県考古学会誌|publisher=栃木県考古学会|volume=|number=21|pages=91-100|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=内山敏行|title=栃木県下野市御鷲山古墳の小札甲|date=2022|journal=研究紀要|publisher=公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=30|pages=89-101|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[下野薬師寺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 御鷲山古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:おわしやまこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}}

The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Owashiyama Kofun}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Miwashi Yama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Owashiyama Kofun]] [[sn:washiyama Kofun]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[en:Owashiyama Kofun]]
