The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site]] was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimotsuke|WD=Q851804}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.405297222222224|N|139.8769111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113774370]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimotsuke|WD=Q851804}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.405297222222224|N|139.8769111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimotsuke|WD=Q851804|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.405297222222224|N|139.8769111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - revision.text:
[[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimotsuke|WD=Q851804|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.405297222222224|N|139.8769111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Owashiyama Kofun | native_name = 御鷲山古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Owashiyama Kofun''' (御鷲山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound is not designated as a historic site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | '''Owashiyama Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:御鷲山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau west of the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It is the largest of over 20 Kofuns forming the Kitahara Kofun Cluster. Surveys of the mound and excavations of the stone chamber have been conducted.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582186a3468fd.pdf Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Explorations #13: Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. It has two tiers, with the lower tier forming a flat platform characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Excavations revealed artifacts such as {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars. The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front and back sections. The chamber opens to the south and measures 12 meters in total length, constructed from cut tuff blocks. Items found in the chamber include iron arrowheads, small knives, armor fragments, and horse trappings. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Nearby, the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Temple Site|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} was later established, making this mound significant for understanding the history of the temple's foundation. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:Historical Site Information Board. * Total length: First tier approx. 83 m, second tier approx. 63 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 28 m ** Height: approx. 5 m (from the top of the first tier) * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 24 m ** Height: approx. 4 m (from the top of the first tier) == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction of the front section. The dimensions are: * Total length: approx. 12 m * Main chamber: approx. 2.8 m in length, approx. 2.0 m wide (at the back wall), approx. 2.0 m in height The chamber's tuff stone construction uses large cut blocks, with river stones laid on the floor.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/582184268c7a1.pdf Owashiyama Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Owashiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ken Takezawa|year=1989|chapter=Owashiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|WD=Q11361399|lt=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|1=Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=39 Owashiyama Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|御鷲山古墳|version=96035283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御鷲山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q113774370}} [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
